messages.content: Please allow two to three business days for your funds to clear once received. This may be longer if further documents are required. the company is not responsible for any loss you may incur due to your funds not being cleared.
Thank you for your understanding.

redraft the clause, fintec company, terms and conditions 
Please allow two to three business days for your funds to clear once received. This may be longer if further documents are required. the company is not responsible for any loss you may incur due to your funds not being cleared. 
responsible

We kindly request your patience as our system processes your funds. Please note that it may take two to three business days for your funds to clear upon receipt. In some cases, if additional documents are needed, the clearance process may take longer. It is important to understand that any loss incurred due to delayed fund clearance is not the responsibility of the company.
draft it in a formal way

Please allow a timeframe of two to three business days for your funds to complete the clearance process once they are received by our company. However, please be aware that in certain instances where additional documentation is required, the clearance period may be extended. We would like to emphasize that the company cannot be held accountable for any financial losses that may be incurred as a result of funds not being cleared within the specified timeframe.

redraft in a formal way, terms and conditions for financial institution: 
When you give us an automatic order via our client portal, we will not automatically make the transfer if the following conditions are not fulfilled:
we must do tax withholding
if the bank details you provide appear incorrect

In order to maintain the utmost compliance with applicable regulations and to ensure the accuracy of our financial transactions, the following conditions must be fulfilled when giving an automatic order via our client portal:

1. Tax Withholding: We reserve the right to withhold taxes as required by relevant tax authorities. Therefore, if tax withholding is necessary, we will not proceed with the automatic transfer until this obligation is met.

2. Accuracy of Bank Details: It is imperative that the bank details provided by you are accurate and complete. In the event that any discrepancies or inconsistencies are detected, we retain the right to withhold the automatic transfer until the correct and verified bank details are provided.

We emphasize that these conditions are in place to safeguard the integrity of our financial operations and to ensure compliance with regulatory standards. By using our client portal for automatic orders, you acknowledge and agree to adhere to these conditions outlined above.
in one clause

In order for us to proceed with an automatic transfer as per your order through our client portal, it is imperative that the following conditions are fulfilled: tax withholding obligations must be met, and the bank details provided must be accurate and valid. Failure to meet these conditions will result in the automatic transfer not being executed.

Since you have give an automatic instruction, they can not be cancelled.
These may be cancelled on the client portal - via section "order preferences - update your order instructions

Once an automatic instruction has been submitted, it cannot be cancelled as it is processed promptly by our system. However, please note that you have the option to manage your automatic instructions through our client portal. You can access the "Order Preferences" section and update your instructions accordingly.
please rewrite these clauses in formal and legal way

1. Once an automatic instruction has been submitted, it becomes irrevocable and cannot be cancelled, as it is promptly processed by our system. 

2. However, we provide you with the ability to manage and modify your automatic instructions through our client portal. Specifically, you can access the dedicated section labeled "Order Preferences" to update your instructions as necessary.

9.	Third Party instructions
9.1	Without derogating from the above, the Client can also give instructions through Third Party. The Company will only allow Client’s authorized person who you have given Power of Attorney to act on your behalf. Power of attorney must be given using the Company’s form (“POA”). No other Power of Attorneys will be acceptable. 
9.2	You will be responsible for all acts or omissions of the person in possession of POA, whether or not you have authorized such acts or omissions, as if they were your own and we shall have no liability to you, the POA or any other third party for any loss, damage or expense incurred due to any act or omission of the POA.
9.3	The Client hereby authorizes the Company to accept, act and rely upon any Instruction that reasonably appears to be an Instruction delivered by a poa. The Company shall not be liable for reasonably relying on an Instruction which was in fact a forgery of a physical document or a forged electronic message. Without limiting the foregoing, the Company has the right, acting reasonably, to decline to accept any Instruction if it has any doubt as to its authenticity.

9. Third-Party Instructions

9.1 The Client has the option to provide instructions through a designated third party. However, the Company will only recognize and allow instructions from individuals who have been authorized by the Client through a valid Power of Attorney ("POA"). The POA must be granted using the specific form provided by the Company, and no other Power of Attorneys will be accepted.

9.2 The Client accepts full responsibility for all actions or omissions of the authorized person holding the POA, regardless of whether such actions or omissions were authorized by the Client. In such cases, the Company shall bear no liability towards the Client, the authorized person holding the POA, or any other third party for any loss, damage, or expenses incurred as a result of the actions or omissions of the authorized person holding the POA.

9.3 By providing the POA, the Client authorizes the Company to accept, act upon, and rely upon any instructions that reasonably appear to be delivered by the authorized person holding the POA. The Company shall not be held liable for reasonably relying on instructions that were later determined to be forgeries of physical documents or forged electronic messages. However, it is understood that the Company retains the right, in its reasonable discretion, to decline any instruction if there are doubts regarding its authenticity.

make this clause stronger and formal 
A “Manifest Error” means a misquote by the company having regard to the current market conditions at the time a Transaction is opened or closed. the Company may, for this purpose, take into account all information including, without limitation, information concerning all relevant market conditions, any error in, or lack of clarity of, any information, source, announcement or obvious misquote from the company’s usual method of calculating exchange rates. The Company shall have the sole and Absolut discretion to close, void or amend any transaction that is affected by a Manifest Error, as defined above. The company shall not be liable for any loss or damage arising from or in connection with any Manifest Error or the company’s exercise of its rights under this clause.

A “Manifest Error” shall be defined as a misquote made by the company, taking into consideration prevailing market conditions at the time a Transaction is initiated or concluded. In determining a Manifest Error, the Company has the authority to consider all available information, including but not limited to relevant market conditions, any errors or ambiguities in provided information, inaccuracies in sources, announcements, or evident misquotations based on the company's established method of calculating exchange rates. Hence, the Company retains the absolute and exclusive discretion to rectify, nullify, or modify any transaction affected by a Manifest Error, as defined above. It is important to note that the company cannot be held accountable for any loss or damage arising directly or indirectly from a Manifest Error or the exercise of its rights under this clause.

please make this clause strong, without liability on the company 
It is the Client's responsibility to ensure receipt of the amounts in the payee account  to which the payments are to be transferred. 
Transfer of payments to third parties are subject to the company's approval in relation to the sender and/or recipient of the payment, in accordance with any law, but the Company shall have no responsibility regarding the destination account and/or beneficiary, including the funds themselves, their holding, use and all matters related. The Company is not and shall not be a party to the transaction and/or any dispute between the Client and the beneficiary/s and shall not be responsible in any way for the quality of the transaction, the products and/or services for which the payments are transferred at the Client's request.
the company will not have lability or will not be responsible to any financial damages

The Client acknowledges and agrees that it is solely their responsibility to ensure the proper receipt of transferred amounts in the designated payee account. Furthermore, any transfer of payments to third parties is subject to the Company's approval, considering both the sender and/or recipient of the payment in accordance with applicable laws. However, it is explicitly stated that the Company assumes no responsibility whatsoever for the destination account and/or beneficiary, including but not limited to the funds themselves, their holding, use, and all related matters. The Company shall not be a party to the transaction being executed and shall bear no responsibility for any disputes between the Client and the beneficiary/beneficiaries involved. Moreover, the Company shall not be held liable or responsible in any manner for the quality of the transaction, nor for the products and/or services for which the payments are being transferred at the explicit request of the Client. Consequently, the Company will not accept any financial damages and shall not be held accountable for any such losses or liabilities incurred by the Client.

redraft the clause in a formal way and to make it more clear 
If you communicate with us through our livechat and/or WhatsApp options on our website, client portal or WhatsApp app, you agree to the terms and conditions of these services. Use of livechat and/or WhatsApp services is subject to the WhatsApp Terms of Service and Privacy Policy https://www.whatsapp.com/legal/business-terms?l. You can learn about the information collected as part of these services, the uses of the information, how to cancel cookies and more in the WhatsApp privacy policy using the link above. By using the livechat and/or WhatsApp options on our website you gives your consent to the WhatsApp policy.  If you fail to provide the correct security questions we will terminate the chat without taking instructions or giving information. 
We will not accept trading instructions via this method.

By engaging in communication with us through our livechat and/or WhatsApp options on our website, client portal, or WhatsApp app, you hereby acknowledge and agree to abide by the terms and conditions governing these services. The utilization of our livechat and/or WhatsApp services is contingent upon acceptance of the WhatsApp Terms of Service and Privacy Policy, which can be found at the following link: https://www.whatsapp.com/legal/business-terms?l. It is essential to review the WhatsApp privacy policy to familiarize yourself with the information collected during the provision of these services, the purposes for which such information is used, as well as instructions on how to manage cookies, and more.

Please be advised that by utilizing the livechat and/or WhatsApp options on our website, you provide consent to adhere to the WhatsApp policy. In the event that you fail to provide accurate security questions during the chat session, we reserve the right to terminate the conversation without accepting any instructions or providing further information. Additionally, please note that trading instructions will not be accepted via this communication method.
